description: Rebuild acquisition discovery and source execution as a measured production system. Procurement is the mature reference path: it already scans live API/browser-backed sources. Commercial discovery is the urgent missing vertical slice: its cron path currently replays static seed_candidates while bounded Exa search exists only in operator-triggered preview flows. First connect scheduled DiscoveryProgram execution to live Exa search and durable Ash/Oban state; then harden shared source contracts, budgets, provider adapters, feedback, E2E proof, compliance, canary rollout, and later governed Pyex workflow harvesting. Preserve Ash as the business boundary and operator review before promotion.

comments.text:
Dependency map:

```mermaid
graph LR
  B[fx2.1 Baseline] --> M[fx2.2 Source contract]
  N[fx2.3 Nix browser] --> J[fx2.4 Jido API] --> R[fx2.5 Retrieval policy]
  B --> E[fx2.6 Exa generation]
  M --> E --> O[fx2.7 Oban durability] --> X[fx2.8 Enrichment] --> F[fx2.9 Feedback]
  F --> W[fx2.10 Websets evaluation]
  R --> G[fx2.11 OpenGov]
  R --> D[fx2.12 BidNet]
  B --> S[fx2.13 SAM budgeting]
  P[6hf Portal inventory] --> C[fx2.14 Source portfolio]
  M --> C
  F --> H[fx2.15 Health routing]
  G --> H
  D --> H
  S --> H
  C --> H
  F --> V[fx2.16 Eval corpus]
  H --> V --> Y[fx2.17 Pyex pilot] --> Z[fx2.18 Harvest governance]
```

Execution guidance: browser foundation and acquisition baseline can run in parallel worktrees. Exa production work waits on the baseline and source contract. Provider adapters converge before automatic portfolio routing. Pyex remains downstream of measured feedback and an offline eval corpus.

Codebase audit and quality steering (2026-07-11):

Evidence:
- Implemented resources were checked against docs/llm/generated/resources.json and config :ash_domains.
- Focused baseline passed 37 tests with PHX_SERVER=false across Exa preview, discovery runner/scheduler, source pipeline/health, and lead-to-pursuit behavior.
- Initial test invocation inherited PHX_SERVER and collided on port 4000; offline CI/E2E beads now require the endpoint disabled explicitly.
- Current discovery production path is still seed_candidates in lib/garden/commercial/discovery_pipeline.ex, while Exa execution lives separately in lib/garden/acquisition/lead_preview.ex.
- DiscoverySchedulerWorker has no direct worker test, and DiscoveryProgram stores cadence but not a full authoritative run lifecycle.
- Acquisition source LiveView builds source bucket/search rules with Ash.Query; this belongs in intent-named resource reads/preparations.
- GnomeGarden.Browser still shells directly to agent-browser; provider paths already include a separate Playwright boundary.
- Existing tests are strong at action/module behavior, but do not yet prove durable provider -> Oban -> candidate/bid -> finding -> review -> signal/pursuit flows under retry and failure.

Quality completion rule for every implementation bead:
1. Search installed docs before editing and record any consequential framework decision.
2. Persist business behavior in Ash resources/actions and expose it through domain code interfaces.
3. Keep meaningful query/filter/load shapes out of LiveViews and orchestration services.
4. Use Ash codegen/migrate and refresh the LLM resource map for resource changes.
5. Keep durable work in AshOban/Oban, composed writes transactional, retries idempotent, and effects bounded.
6. Add focused success, failure, authorization, retry, and duplicate-delivery tests; provider tests stay fixture-backed and offline.
7. Run format/checks and the narrowest relevant tests before the mid-epic quality gate; run the full CI profile before canary.
8. Do not add a Jido agent runtime, raw Repo access, hidden metadata contracts, or autonomous promotion.

Sequencing summary:
```mermaid
graph LR
  A[Baseline + contract] --> M[Migration + budgets]
  B[Nix + Jido facade] --> R[Retrieval policy]
  R --> F[Provider fixture contracts]
  M --> X[Exa durable path]
  F --> X
  F --> P[Provider adapters]
  X --> Q[Feedback + routing]
  P --> Q
  Q --> G[Mid-epic Ash quality gate]
  G --> E1[Exa E2E]
  G --> E2[Procurement E2E]
  G --> E3[Review-to-pursuit E2E]
  E1 --> I[Failure injection]
  E2 --> I
  E3 --> I
  I --> O[Runbook + operator validation]
  C[Compliance] --> O
  O --> K[Canary/shadow]
  K --> U[Measured pilot]
  U --> L[Retire legacy paths]
```

title: Rebuild acquisition discovery and source execution
